items.summary: api.PatternSubstitution @deprecated points to utils.PatternSubstitution but escapeForRegex lives in utils.CqlUtils

evidence: escapeForRegex delegates to pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils.CqlUtils.escapeForRegex — not to utils.PatternSubstitution, which does not define escapeForRegex.
A developer following the migration note would look for escapeForRegex in utils.PatternSubstitution and not find it.
detail.suggestion: Update the class-level deprecation note to reflect that escapeForRegex migrated to utils.CqlUtils: '@deprecated substituteCollocate and extractXposFromConstraint: use utils.PatternSubstitution. escapeForRegex: use utils.CqlUtils.escapeForRegex.'

items.summary: api/PatternSubstitution.java is a deprecated delegation-only wrapper that belongs to api/ (HTTP handlers), polluting its ownership boundary

evidence: api/PatternSubstitution.java carries @Deprecated and is a pure delegate to utils/PatternSubstitution (all three public methods forward directly).
The api package otherwise contains only HTTP handler classes (WordSketchApiServer, SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, HttpApiUtils) — CQL pattern utilities have no business there.
A parallel test file src/test/.../api/PatternSubstitutionTest.java exists alongside the deprecated class, meaning the zombie tests an already-moved concept.
detail.suggestion: Delete api/PatternSubstitution.java and its test file once SketchHandlers (the only remaining caller inside api/) is confirmed to import utils/PatternSubstitution directly (it already does). Verify no external callers reference the api-package version before deletion.

items.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser exposes three near-identical thin wrappers (extractLemmaFromMatch, extractCollocateLemma, extractLemmaAt) that all ultimately call extractLastLemma or extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, making call-site choice non-obvious

evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch(String matchText): calls extractLastLemma and lowercases result
extractCollocateLemma(String matchXml): calls extractLastLemma, preserves original case — differs from extractLemmaFromMatch only in lowercasing
extractLemmaAt(String xml, int position): delegates to extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition — an alias with no added behavior
The distinction between 'fromMatch', 'collocateLemma', and 'lemmaAt' is unclear from names alone; callers must read all three implementations to choose correctly
detail.suggestion: Collapse to two clearly named public methods: `extractLastLemmaLower(String xml)` (replaces extractLemmaFromMatch) and `extractLastLemma(String xml)` (replaces extractCollocateLemma, made package-visible or public as needed). Remove extractLemmaAt as a redundant alias for extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition and call extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition directly at call sites. Update callers in BlackLabQueryExecutor accordingly.

items.summary: api/PatternSubstitution.java is a lingering delegation stub that blurs the api/utils seam

evidence: api/PatternSubstitution is annotated @Deprecated and its three methods each delegate 1-for-1 to utils/PatternSubstitution or utils/CqlUtils with no added logic.
A developer arriving at the api/ package sees PatternSubstitution and must check whether to use the api or utils version — both exist for the same purpose.
detail.suggestion: Delete api/PatternSubstitution.java. Verify with grep that no callers reference it (it has no internal callers beyond the delegation stubs themselves), then remove the file to complete the migration to utils/PatternSubstitution.

If you cannot fix an item, report it and move to the next one.
For cluster items: if the autofix_hint finds 0 results, drill into individual issues with the primary_command.
